facts.text: Wayne Gretzky holds 61 NHL records, including most career goals (894), assists (1,963), and points (2,857).
The Stanley Cup is the oldest professional sports trophy in North America, first awarded in 1893.
The Montreal Canadiens have won the most Stanley Cup championships with 24 titles.
Wayne Gretzky's number 99 is the only number retired league-wide by the NHL.
A regulation NHL puck is made of vulcanized rubber, weighs 6 ounces, and is 1 inch thick and 3 inches in diameter.
The 'Miracle on Ice' in the 1980 Winter Olympics saw the U.S. amateur team defeat the heavily favored Soviet Union 4-3.
Bobby Orr revolutionized the defenseman position by playing an offensive style and won 8 consecutive Norris Trophies.
The original six NHL teams were the Canadiens, Maple Leafs, Red Wings, Blackhawks, Bruins, and Rangers.
Mario Lemieux scored 5 goals in 5 different ways in a single game in 1988: even strength, power play, shorthanded, penalty shot, and empty net.
A Zamboni ice resurfacing machine takes about 7 minutes to resurface an NHL rink and uses 150 gallons of water.
Gordie Howe played professional hockey for an astounding 32 seasons, from 1946 to 1980.
A 'Gordie Howe hat trick' consists of a goal, an assist, and a fight in the same game.
The NHL expanded from 6 to 12 teams in 1967, doubling the league's size in one season.
Sidney Crosby was the first overall pick in the 2005 NHL Draft and won the Stanley Cup three times with the Pittsburgh Penguins.
The fastest recorded slapshot in NHL history was 108.8 mph by Zdeno Chara at the 2012 All-Star Skills Competition.
A standard NHL rink is 200 feet long and 85 feet wide, smaller than an Olympic-sized rink (200 x 100 feet).
Patrick Roy won the Conn Smythe Trophy (playoff MVP) three times, more than any other player.
The 'Conn Smythe Trophy' is named after the legendary Toronto Maple Leafs founder and is awarded to the MVP of the NHL playoffs.
Alexander Ovechkin has been chasing Wayne Gretzky's all-time goal record of 894, becoming one of the greatest goal-scorers ever.
The longest NHL game ever played was a 1936 playoff game between the Detroit Red Wings and Montreal Maroons, lasting 176 minutes of overtime.
Hat tricks — three goals by one player in a game — are celebrated by fans throwing hats onto the ice.
Martin Brodeur holds the NHL record for most career wins by a goaltender with 691.
The 'Great One' was Wayne Gretzky's nickname, given to him because of his unprecedented dominance of the sport.
NHL goalie masks were not commonly worn until Jacques Plante popularized them in 1959 after taking a puck to the face.
The Edmonton Oilers won five Stanley Cups in seven years during the 1980s dynasty led by Wayne Gretzky.
A hockey stick curve cannot exceed ¾ of an inch according to NHL rules.
The Detroit Red Wings' octopus-throwing tradition dates back to 1952, symbolizing the 8 wins needed to capture the Cup at that time.
Bobby Hull's slapshot was once measured at over 118 mph and terrified goalies who played without masks.
The NHL adopted the shootout to decide regular-season games tied after overtime starting in the 2005-06 season.
Connor McDavid is widely considered the fastest skater in NHL history, regularly clocking speeds over 22 mph.
The penalty box is sometimes called the 'sin bin' — players serve 2 minutes for minor penalties and 5 for majors.
The Philadelphia Flyers' 'Broad Street Bullies' of the 1970s were known for their incredibly physical and intimidating style of play.
Mark Messier is the only player to captain two different teams to Stanley Cup championships (Oilers and Rangers).
A hockey goal is 6 feet wide and 4 feet tall, with a net that extends about 40 inches deep.
The 'Curse of 1940' haunted the New York Rangers, who didn't win the Stanley Cup from 1940 to 1994 — a 54-year drought.
Jaromir Jagr played in the NHL until age 45 and is second all-time in points after Wayne Gretzky.
The NHL's 3-on-3 overtime format was introduced in 2015-16 to reduce the number of games decided by shootouts.
Dominik Hasek, 'The Dominator,' won six Vezina Trophies as the league's best goaltender.
The 'Icing' rule, which forbids shooting the puck from behind center ice across the opposing goal line, was introduced in 1937.
The Vegas Golden Knights reached the Stanley Cup Final in their inaugural season in 2017-18, an unprecedented feat.
Hockey players lose an average of 5-8 pounds of water weight during a single game.
The 'Offsides' rule in hockey means an attacking player cannot enter the offensive zone before the puck.
Teemu Selanne scored 76 goals as a rookie in 1992-93, setting the all-time NHL rookie goals record.
The Conn Smythe Trophy has been awarded to a player from the losing team five times in its history.
NHL players go through an average of 12 sticks per game team-wide.
Ron Hextall was the first NHL goalie to score a goal by shooting the puck into the opposing net.
The 'Neutral Zone Trap' defensive system was perfected by the New Jersey Devils in the mid-1990s, frustrating opposing offenses.
The Hart Trophy for NHL MVP has been awarded since 1924, making it one of the oldest player awards in North American sports.
NHL teams play 82 regular-season games, split evenly between 41 home games and 41 away games.
The 'Original Six' era from 1942 to 1967 is considered hockey's golden age, with intense rivalries among just six teams.
Paul Coffey was the fastest skating defenseman of his era and holds the record for most goals by a defenseman in a single season (48).
The Stanley Cup weighs about 35 pounds and stands 35.25 inches tall.
Each player on the winning team gets to spend a day with the Stanley Cup during the summer, leading to many famous Cup photos.
The 'Calder Trophy' is awarded to the NHL's best rookie each season and has been given since 1933.
NHL goaltenders can play the puck behind the net only within a designated trapezoid area, a rule added in 2005.
Maurice 'Rocket' Richard was the first NHL player to score 50 goals in a season, accomplishing the feat in 1944-45.
The 'Power Play' occurs when one team has a numerical advantage due to the opposing team having a player in the penalty box.
Leon Draisaitl became one of the premier centers in hockey, consistently scoring over 100 points per season.
The NHL Winter Classic, an outdoor regular-season game, has been played annually since 2008.
Goaltender pads were significantly reduced in size by NHL rules in 2005 to increase scoring.
Guy Lafleur's flowing hair streaming behind him as he skated became one of hockey's most iconic images.
The 'Lady Byng Trophy' is awarded to the NHL player who best combines sportsmanship and skill.
A standard NHL period is 20 minutes of playing time, with three periods per game.
The 'Selke Trophy' for the best defensive forward has been awarded since 1978.
Auston Matthews scored 4 goals in his NHL debut in 2016, the first player in modern history to do so.
Nicklas Lidstrom won 7 Norris Trophies and is considered one of the greatest European players in NHL history.
The Pittsburgh Penguins selected Sidney Crosby first overall in 2005 after winning the draft lottery.
Henrik Lundqvist made 459 career wins as a Ranger, making him the franchise's all-time winningest goalie.
The 'Slap Shot' movie from 1977 starring Paul Newman is considered the greatest hockey movie ever made.
The AHL (American Hockey League) serves as the primary development league for the NHL.
Jean Beliveau won 10 Stanley Cups as a player with the Montreal Canadiens.
Slapshots can travel at speeds exceeding 100 mph, making hockey one of the fastest sports in the world.
The 'Face-off' starts each period and restarts play after every stoppage, with centers from each team battling for the puck.
Cale Makar won the Norris Trophy as the NHL's best defenseman and a Stanley Cup championship before age 25.
The first NHL game was played on December 19, 1917 between the Montreal Canadiens and the Ottawa Senators.
Phil Esposito was the first NHL player to score 76 goals in a season (1970-71) before Gretzky shattered the record.
NHL teams employ equipment managers who sharpen skates before every game — most players prefer a specific hollow radius.
The 'Five Hole' refers to the space between a goaltender's legs, one of the five target areas shooters aim for.
The Tampa Bay Lightning won back-to-back Stanley Cups in 2020 and 2021 during the COVID-era bubble and regular playoffs.
Bobby Orr's iconic flying goal in the 1970 Stanley Cup Final is one of the most photographed moments in sports history.
Since 2005, every NHL game that is tied after regulation goes to a 5-minute 3-on-3 overtime period.
The 'Playoff Beard' tradition — not shaving during the postseason — has been a hockey tradition since the 1980s.
Henrik and Daniel Sedin, identical twins from Sweden, played their entire 17-year NHL careers together on the Vancouver Canucks.
The NHL uses a salary cap system that was implemented after the 2004-05 lockout cancelled the entire season.
An NHL season typically runs from October through June when including the Stanley Cup Playoffs.
The 'Zamboni' ice resurfacer was invented by Frank Zamboni in 1949 and revolutionized ice maintenance.
Mike Bossy scored 50 goals in 50 games in 1981, matching Maurice Richard's legendary pace.
The Chicago Blackhawks won three Stanley Cups in six years (2010, 2013, 2015) during their modern dynasty run.
A hockey rink's ice is typically only about ¾ of an inch thick and maintained at about 22°F (-5.5°C).
The 'Two-Line Pass' rule was eliminated in 2005, opening up the game and allowing longer stretch passes.
Steve Yzerman captained the Detroit Red Wings for 19 seasons, the longest tenure as captain for one franchise.
Nathan MacKinnon is one of the fastest players in the modern NHL and helped Colorado win the Cup in 2022.
The 'Enforcer' role — a player whose primary job was fighting — has largely disappeared from modern hockey.
Brett Hull scored 86 goals in 1990-91, the third-highest single-season total in NHL history.
The Calder Cup is awarded to the AHL champion, while the Stanley Cup goes to the NHL champion.
Carey Price won the Hart Trophy and Vezina Trophy in 2015, cementing his status as one of the best goalies of his generation.
The NHL Entry Draft allows teams to select eligible prospects, with the team with the worst record getting the best odds for the first pick.
Tim Horton, the famous Canadian donut chain founder, was also a Hall of Fame NHL defenseman who played 24 seasons.
The 'Suspension Wheel' is a fan term mocking the seemingly inconsistent supplemental discipline from the NHL's Department of Player Safety.
Over 40% of NHL players are from Canada, though the percentage of European and American players has steadily increased.
The 'Coast-to-Coast' goal describes a player skating the entire length of the ice and scoring, one of hockey's most exciting individual feats.
Hockey skate blades are only about 1/8 of an inch wide, requiring incredible balance and edge control.
Ray Bourque's trade from the Boston Bruins to the Colorado Avalanche in 2000, culminating in a Cup win, is one of hockey's great stories.
The 'Crease' is the blue-painted semicircle in front of each goal where the goaltender operates.
Jack Adams Award for NHL Coach of the Year has been given since 1974.
The Florida Panthers won their first Stanley Cup in franchise history in 2024.
Wayne Gretzky scored 92 goals in the 1981-82 season, a record that may never be broken.
The 'Spinarama' is a 360-degree spin move used by players to evade defenders or goalies.
Igor Shesterkin has become one of the best goaltenders in the NHL, earning Vezina Trophy recognition.
The 'Penalty Kill' occurs when a shorthanded team must defend against the opposing team's power play.
NHL arenas typically seat between 17,000 and 21,000 fans for hockey games.
The 'Art Ross Trophy' is awarded to the player who leads the NHL in total points (goals + assists) during the regular season.
Darryl Sittler holds the NHL record for most points in a single game with 10 (6 goals, 4 assists) on February 7, 1976.
The 'Backhand' shot is one of the most difficult shots in hockey because it requires flipping the blade of the stick.
The Ottawa Senators' original franchise won 11 Stanley Cups before folding in 1934. The current franchise began play in 1992.
The 'Puck Drop' ceremonially begins games and is often performed by a guest of honor, similar to a first pitch in baseball.
Ilya Bryzgalov became famous for his quirky personality and philosophical musings in the HBO documentary '24/7.'
The Seattle Kraken became the NHL's 32nd franchise in 2021-22.
A 'Dangle' is a deft stickhandling move used to beat a defender one-on-one.
The 'Presidents' Trophy' is awarded to the team with the best regular-season record, but it's considered somewhat jinxed in the playoffs.
Hockey gloves weigh about 14-16 ounces and feature heavy padding on the back of the hand and fingers.
Joe Thornton played over 1,700 NHL games and recorded over 1,500 career points.
The 'Wrist Shot' is the most commonly used shot in hockey, prized for its accuracy and quick release.
Andrei Vasilevskiy won the Conn Smythe Trophy in 2021, closing out every series in the playoffs with a shutout.
The 'Blue Line' divides the ice into three zones: defensive, neutral, and offensive.
Kirk McLean's 'Save of the Century' in the 1994 Stanley Cup Final against Robert Reichel's breakaway remains iconic.
The NHL has played outdoor games at venues including Dodger Stadium, Michigan Stadium, and Fenway Park.
Ken Dryden won 6 Stanley Cups and wrote the acclaimed book 'The Game' about his experiences as an NHL goaltender.
A 'One-Timer' is a shot taken directly off a pass without stopping the puck first, generating extra power.
The 'Trade Deadline' in the NHL typically falls in early March, when contending teams acquire players for playoff runs.
Mats Sundin was the first European player to be selected first overall in the NHL Draft in 1989.
The 'Wraparound' is a play where a player skates behind the net and tries to jam the puck in on the goalie's far side.
Curtis Joseph, 'CuJo,' made 454 career wins despite never being drafted by an NHL team.
The NHL's cap hit system means players' salaries are averaged over the length of their contract for cap purposes.
Grant Fuhr was one of the first Black stars in the NHL and backstopped the Edmonton Oilers dynasty in the 1980s.
The 'Hash Marks' on the ice indicate where players must line up during face-offs in the offensive and defensive zones.
The 2004-05 NHL lockout was the first time a major North American sports league cancelled an entire season.
Erik Karlsson has been one of the most offensively dynamic defensemen of his generation, twice leading all blueliners in scoring.
The 'Delay of Game' penalty is assessed when a defending player deliberately shoots the puck over the glass from their defensive zone.
Mark Scheifele, Kyle Connor, and the Winnipeg Jets have been consistent playoff contenders in the modern NHL.
Peter Forsberg was known as 'Foppa' and his iconic penalty shot in the 1994 Olympics became a Swedish postage stamp.
The 'Deke' is another term for a fake or feint move used to beat a goaltender or defender.
The Carolina Hurricanes' post-game 'Storm Surge' celebrations became one of the most entertaining traditions in modern hockey.
Al MacInnis had one of the hardest slapshots in NHL history, winning the hardest shot competition seven times at the All-Star Game.
The 'Butterfly' goaltending style — dropping to the knees with pads spread — became dominant in the 1990s and 2000s.
Hockey tape is used extensively by players to wrap their stick blade and handle for better puck control and grip.
Pavel Bure, 'The Russian Rocket,' scored 437 goals despite injuries shortening what would have been a legendary career.
The 'Red Line' at center ice divides the rink in half and establishes the icing rule boundaries.
P.K. Subban's personality and charitable work made him one of hockey's most marketable and recognizable players.
The NHL draft lottery uses a weighted system where the worst teams have the highest probability of getting the first pick.
Scott Stevens was one of the most feared hitters in NHL history, delivering massive open-ice body checks.
Filip Forsberg scored the most overtime goals in a single playoff year in 2023, earning 'Playoff Forsberg' fame.
The 'Boarding' penalty is called when a player drives an opponent dangerously into the boards.
The NHL's HockeyFightsDotCom tracked over 30,000 fights in league history before fighting decreased dramatically.
Jarome Iginla was one of the greatest Black hockey players and scored 625 career goals.
The 'Toe Drag' is a stickhandling move where a player pulls the puck back with the toe of the blade to avoid a defender's stick.
Connor Bedard was the first overall pick in the 2023 NHL Draft and scored highlight-reel goals as an 18-year-old.
The NHL Entry Draft is held in late June or early July and consists of seven rounds.
Jonathan Toews captained the Chicago Blackhawks to three Stanley Cups (2010, 2013, 2015) and won the Conn Smythe in 2010.
The 'ECHL' (formerly East Coast Hockey League) is a mid-level professional league below the AHL.
Wayne Gretzky was traded from the Edmonton Oilers to the Los Angeles Kings on August 9, 1988, shocking the entire hockey world.
Roberto Luongo was famous for his sense of humor on social media, becoming one of the first NHL players known for Twitter.
The 'Crossbar Challenge' is a common hockey drill and competition where players try to hit the crossbar from center ice.
The 'plus-minus' statistic tracks whether a player is on the ice for more goals scored by his team or against.
Victor Hedman won the Norris Trophy as the NHL's best defenseman and anchored the Tampa Bay Lightning's championship defense.
The Great Western Forum, Madison Square Garden, and the Montreal Forum are among the most iconic arenas in hockey history.
The 'Trap' defensive system, used extensively by the New Jersey Devils, contributed to the NHL's low-scoring era of the late 1990s and early 2000s.
David Pastrnak became one of the NHL's most prolific goal scorers, twice reaching the 60-goal plateau.
In ice hockey, each team has 6 players on the ice: 3 forwards, 2 defensemen, and 1 goaltender.
The 'Stretch Pass' sends the puck from the defensive zone to a forward near the red line for a quick breakout.
The NWHL (now PHF/PWHL) launched as the first women's professional hockey league to pay players a salary in 2015.
A 'Shut Out' occurs when a goaltender allows zero goals in a complete game.
The modern composite hockey stick, made of carbon fiber, has largely replaced wooden sticks since the early 2000s.
Brian Leetch was the first American-born player to win the Conn Smythe Trophy in 1994.
The 'Breakaway' is one of hockey's most exciting plays — a player skating alone toward the opposing goaltender with no defenders between them.
Zdeno Chara, at 6'9", was the tallest player in NHL history and had one of the league's hardest slapshots.
The 'Icing' whistle stops play and results in a face-off in the offending team's defensive zone.
The 'Corsi' statistic measures shot attempts (for and against) to evaluate a player's or team's puck possession.
Ryan O'Reilly won the Conn Smythe Trophy in 2019 after leading the St. Louis Blues to their first Stanley Cup.
The NHL All-Star Game has varied its format over the years, including a 3-on-3 tournament and traditional game.
Jack Eichel's artificial disc replacement surgery in 2021 was a first in NHL history and sparked debate about player autonomy.
The PWHL (Professional Women's Hockey League) launched in January 2024 with six teams across North America.
Eric Lindros was drafted first overall in 1991 but refused to play for the Quebec Nordiques, resulting in a blockbuster trade to Philadelphia.
The 'Saucer Pass' is an aerial pass that travels just above the ice surface, hopping over defenders' sticks.
Each Stanley Cup champion gets their players' names engraved on the Cup, and when a ring fills up, the oldest one is retired to the Hall of Fame.
The 'Clapper' is a nickname for the slapshot, named after early hockey star Dit Clapper.
Jeremy Roenick scored 513 career goals and was known for his charismatic personality and outspoken media appearances.
The 'High Stick' penalty is called when a player's stick makes contact with an opponent above the shoulders.
In the Stanley Cup Playoffs, the first team to win 4 games in a best-of-7 series advances to the next round.
Gilbert Perreault, Rick Martin, and Rene Robert formed the Buffalo Sabres' legendary 'French Connection' line.
The 'Slot' is the area directly in front of the goal between the face-off circles — the most dangerous scoring zone on the ice.
The 'Gordie Howe Bridge,' connecting Detroit and Windsor, is named after the hockey legend who was beloved in both the U.S. and Canada.
Alex Ovechkin became only the third player in NHL history to score 800 career goals in 2023.
Terry Sawchuk recorded 103 career shutouts, an NHL record that still stands.
The 'Blocked Shot' stat has become increasingly important as teams adopt shot-blocking defensive strategies.
The Tampa Bay Lightning's salary cap circumvention using long-term injured reserve sparked controversy during their championship runs.
Luc Robitaille is the highest-scoring left winger in NHL history with 668 career goals.
A 'Power Forward' in hockey is a large forward who combines physical play with scoring ability, like Cam Neely or Jarome Iginla.
The 'Line Change' in hockey happens during play, with players hopping on and off the ice — a unique aspect of the sport.
NHL players typically sharpen their skates between every period, with each player having a preferred blade hollow.
Mario Lemieux retired, came back, retired again, and then became owner of the Pittsburgh Penguins.
The 'No Goal' call — when a goal is waved off by officials — is one of the most controversial calls in hockey.
Wayne Gretzky's trade to the Los Angeles Kings is credited with popularizing hockey in the American Sun Belt.
The 'Wraparound' attempt requires a player to gain speed behind the net and try to stuff the puck past the goalie on the far side.
Martin St. Louis was undrafted and stood only 5'8" but won a Hart Trophy and a Stanley Cup.
The 'Interference' penalty is called when a player impedes the movement of an opponent who doesn't have the puck.
The Stanley Cup has been to the bottom of a swimming pool, on top of a mountain, and even to a war zone.
Chris Chelios played in the NHL until age 48, one of the longest careers in league history.
A goaltender's catching glove is called a 'trapper' and the blocking pad is called a 'blocker.'
The 'Emergency Backup Goaltender' (EBUG) rule allows teams to dress a local amateur goalie if both rostered goalies are injured.
David Ayres, a 42-year-old Zamboni driver, became an EBUG and won a game for the Carolina Hurricanes in 2020.
The Ted Lindsay Award is voted on by the players themselves to recognize the most outstanding player in the NHL.
Sidney Crosby's 'Golden Goal' in overtime of the 2010 Olympic gold medal game against the U.S. is one of Canada's greatest sports moments.
The 'Crease Violation' rule has been controversial — goals have been disallowed for an attacking player's skate being in the crease.
The 'Trapezoid' behind the net restricts where goalies can play the puck and was introduced after the 2004-05 lockout.
Wayne Gretzky's last NHL game was on April 18, 1999, ending the career of the greatest hockey player of all time.
Connor McDavid won three consecutive Art Ross Trophies (2021-2023) and has been the league's most dominant player since his debut.
Auston Matthews became the first player in Toronto Maple Leafs history to score 60 goals in a season, reaching 69 in 2023-24.
Nathan MacKinnon won the Hart Trophy as MVP in 2024 after posting a career-high 140 points for the Colorado Avalanche.
Cale Makar recorded over 20 goals and 70 points in multiple seasons, redefining what a modern NHL defenseman can do offensively.
Leon Draisaitl won the Hart Trophy and Art Ross Trophy in 2020, becoming the first German-born player to win either award.
Jack Hughes was selected first overall by the New Jersey Devils in 2019 and blossomed into a point-per-game superstar by his fourth NHL season.
Matthew Tkachuk was traded from Calgary to Florida in 2022 and led the Panthers to back-to-back Stanley Cup Finals in 2023 and 2024.
Brady Tkachuk became the youngest captain in Ottawa Senators history when he was named captain at age 22 in 2021.
Tyler Seguin was traded from the Boston Bruins to the Dallas Stars in 2013 and became one of the top centers in the Western Conference.
John Tavares signed with his hometown Toronto Maple Leafs as a free agent in 2018 after nine seasons as captain of the New York Islanders.
The Tkachuk family features father Keith, who scored 538 NHL goals, and sons Matthew and Brady, both NHL captains with different teams.
The Howe family made history when Gordie Howe played alongside sons Mark and Marty on the Hartford Whalers in the 1979-80 season.
The Sutter family sent six brothers to the NHL — Brent, Brian, Darryl, Duane, Rich, and Ron — an all-time family record.
The Staal brothers — Eric, Jordan, Marc, and Jared — all played in the NHL, with Eric, Jordan, and Marc playing together on the Carolina Hurricanes.
Bobby Hull and son Brett Hull both scored over 600 NHL goals, making them the highest-scoring father-son duo in league history.
Gordie Howe and son Mark Howe are the only father-son pair both inducted into the Hockey Hall of Fame.
Terry Sawchuk played 21 NHL seasons and was known for his crouching goaltending style; his face bore over 400 stitches from puck impacts.
Glenn Hall played 502 consecutive complete games in goal — a record considered one of the most unbreakable in professional sports.
Tony Esposito won the Vezina Trophy three times with the Chicago Blackhawks and pioneered the butterfly goaltending technique.
Ed Belfour won the Calder Trophy as rookie of the year and the Vezina Trophy in 1991, posting a 2.47 GAA in his debut season.
Mike Richter backstopped the New York Rangers to the 1994 Stanley Cup and was the MVP of the 1996 World Cup of Hockey for Team USA.
Ryan Miller was named MVP of the 2010 Winter Olympics after leading Team USA to the gold medal game, earning a .946 save percentage.
Tuukka Rask posted a .929 career save percentage, one of the highest in NHL history, while backstopping the Bruins for over a decade.
Scotty Bowman won a record 9 Stanley Cups as a head coach with the Canadiens, Penguins, and Red Wings.
Joel Quenneville won three Stanley Cups coaching the Chicago Blackhawks (2010, 2013, 2015), building one of the great modern dynasties.
Mike Babcock became the youngest coach to win the Stanley Cup when he led the Detroit Red Wings to the title in 2008 at age 44.
Jon Cooper coached the Tampa Bay Lightning to back-to-back Stanley Cup championships in 2020 and 2021.
Peter Laviolette has coached five different NHL teams and guided the Carolina Hurricanes to the Stanley Cup in 2006.
Toe Blake coached the Montreal Canadiens to 8 Stanley Cups in 13 seasons, including 5 consecutive titles from 1956 to 1960.
Pat Quinn coached Team Canada to Olympic gold in 2002 and was known as a fierce competitor behind the bench.
The Hartford Whalers relocated to become the Carolina Hurricanes in 1997, and their logo remains one of the most beloved in hockey history.
The Quebec Nordiques moved to Denver in 1995 and became the Colorado Avalanche, winning the Stanley Cup in their very first season.
The original Winnipeg Jets relocated to Phoenix in 1996, becoming the Coyotes, before the NHL returned a new franchise to Winnipeg in 2011.
The Atlanta Thrashers lasted only 12 seasons before relocating to Winnipeg in 2011 to become the current Jets.
The Kansas City Scouts became the Colorado Rockies in 1976 and then the New Jersey Devils in 1982, where they won three Stanley Cups.
The Cleveland Barons merged with the Minnesota North Stars in 1978, making them the only NHL franchise to fold due to financial difficulties in the modern era.
The Minnesota North Stars relocated to Dallas in 1993 and won the Stanley Cup as the Stars in 1999.
Madison Square Garden, home of the New York Rangers, is known as 'The World's Most Famous Arena' and has hosted hockey since 1926.
The Bell Centre in Montreal seats 21,302 for hockey, the largest capacity in the NHL, and the crowd famously sings 'Ole Ole Ole' during wins.
Scotiabank Arena (formerly Air Canada Centre) in Toronto is one of the hardest tickets in sports due to the Maple Leafs' devoted fanbase.
T-Mobile Arena in Las Vegas opened in 2016 and became famous for its elaborate medieval knight pre-game shows for the Golden Knights.
The old Chicago Stadium was known as 'The Madhouse on Madison' for its deafening crowd noise before the Blackhawks moved to the United Center.
Joe Louis Arena in Detroit was home to the Red Wings from 1979 to 2017, replaced by Little Caesars Arena.
The Saddledome in Calgary has a distinctive saddle-shaped roof and has been home to the Flames since 1983.
The 1994 Stanley Cup Final between the New York Rangers and Vancouver Canucks went to 7 games, with Mark Messier guaranteeing a Game 6 win.
The 2011 Stanley Cup Final between Boston and Vancouver went 7 games and ended with riots in Vancouver after the Canucks' loss.
The 'Easter Epic' of 1987 saw the New York Islanders and Washington Capitals play into a fourth overtime period before Pat LaFontaine scored the winner.
In the 2014 playoffs, the Los Angeles Kings became the first team to win three Game 7s on the road in a single postseason en route to the Cup.
The 1993 Montreal Canadiens won a record 10 consecutive overtime games during their improbable Stanley Cup run.
The 2004 Stanley Cup Final ended controversially when the Calgary Flames claimed the puck crossed the goal line in Game 6, but no goal was awarded.
The 1996 Colorado Avalanche swept the Florida Panthers in the Stanley Cup Final, with the series notable for the rat-throwing tradition in Miami.
The Detroit Red Wings and Colorado Avalanche rivalry of the late 1990s featured brawls, including the infamous Claude Lemieux-Darren McCarty fight.
The Bruins-Canadiens rivalry is the oldest in the NHL, with the teams having met in 34 postseason series.
The 2019 St. Louis Blues went from last place in the NHL in January to winning the franchise's first Stanley Cup in June.
Wood hockey sticks, once made from ash and later one-piece yellow birch, dominated the sport until composite sticks took over in the early 2000s.
The first composite hockey sticks cost over $200 each but offered significant improvements in shot power and weight over wood models.
Modern NHL skates weigh about 1.5 to 2 pounds each and use carbon fiber boots with stainless steel or coated blades.
Early hockey helmets were not mandatory until 1979, when the NHL required all new players to wear them — existing players could be grandfathered in.
Craig MacTavish was the last NHL player to play without a helmet, finally retiring in 1997.
Goalie pads evolved from simple leather cricket pads in the early 1900s to today's lightweight synthetic foam and nylon designs.
Modern goaltender masks feature custom airbrushed artwork, a tradition started by Gerry Cheevers, who painted stitches on his mask where pucks would have cut him.
The original hockey pucks were made from frozen cow dung before rubber replaced them in the 1870s.
NHL players wear shin guards made of molded plastic and foam that protect from the knee to the ankle against puck impacts and stick slashes.
Goaltenders in the NHL wear chest protectors that can weigh up to 5 pounds and feature layered foam for blocking shots traveling over 100 mph.
The IIHF World Championship is held every May and has been contested since 1920, with Canada and Russia historically dominating.
The World Junior Championship (WJC), held annually around Christmas and New Year, is one of hockey's most popular international events, especially in Canada.
Canada has won the most IIHF World Junior Championship gold medals, with the tournament serving as a showcase for future NHL stars.
The KHL (Kontinental Hockey League) is the top professional league in Russia and the second-most competitive hockey league in the world after the NHL.
The KHL features teams from Russia, Finland, China, and other nations, with notable alumni including Ilya Kovalchuk and Artemi Panarin.
The Summit Series of 1972 between Canada and the Soviet Union was one of the most dramatic events in hockey history, culminating in Paul Henderson's Game 8 winner.
The Canada Cup tournament (1976-1991) featured the best players from all nations and produced some of the greatest hockey ever played.
The Spengler Cup, contested in Davos, Switzerland since 1923, is the oldest invitational ice hockey tournament in the world.
Sweden has won multiple IIHF World Championship gold medals and consistently produces elite NHL talent like Forsberg, Sundin, and the Sedins.
Finland won Olympic gold in men's hockey for the first time in 2022, defeating the ROC in the final at the Beijing Games.
The first NHL All-Star Game was held in 1947 as a benefit for injured Toronto Maple Leafs player Ace Bailey.
The NHL All-Star Skills Competition features events like fastest skater, hardest shot, accuracy shooting, and the breakaway challenge.
The NHL shifted to a divisional 3-on-3 All-Star tournament format in 2016, replacing the traditional conference vs. conference game.
Wayne Gretzky holds the record for most All-Star Game appearances with 18, showcasing his sustained excellence.
The 'Fenwick' stat is similar to Corsi but excludes blocked shots, focusing only on unblocked shot attempts.
Expected goals (xG) is an analytics metric that evaluates the quality of scoring chances based on shot location, type, and game situation.
The rise of hockey analytics led NHL teams to create analytics departments, with the Toronto Maple Leafs being early adopters under Brendan Shanahan.
Zone entry tracking in hockey analytics reveals that carrying the puck into the offensive zone generates significantly more scoring chances than dump-and-chase.
Game Score, developed by hockey analyst Dom Luszczyszyn, combines multiple stats into a single number to evaluate a player's overall game performance.
The OHL (Ontario Hockey League) has produced more NHL first-overall draft picks than any other junior league.
The WHL (Western Hockey League) spans teams across Western Canada and the Pacific Northwest of the United States.
The QMJHL (Quebec Major Junior Hockey League) is known for developing skilled, offensive players in the francophone junior hockey tradition.
The Memorial Cup is awarded annually to the champion of Canadian major junior hockey, contested among the OHL, WHL, and QMJHL champions.
The AHL has teams in both the United States and Canada, with every NHL franchise having at least one AHL affiliate.
The ECHL serves as the 'AA' level of North American hockey, one step below the AHL, and has teams primarily in smaller U.S. markets.
The CHL (Canadian Hockey League) is the umbrella organization overseeing the OHL, WHL, and QMJHL, producing the majority of NHL draft picks each year.
Hayley Wickenheiser is considered the greatest female hockey player ever, winning four Olympic gold medals and seven World Championship golds for Canada.
Marie-Philip Poulin, known as 'Captain Clutch,' scored the gold medal-winning goals in both the 2010 and 2014 Winter Olympics for Team Canada.
Hilary Knight has been the face of American women's hockey, winning multiple World Championship gold medals and Olympic silver medals for Team USA.
The U.S. women's hockey team ended Canada's Olympic gold medal streak by winning gold at the 2018 PyeongChang Games in a dramatic shootout.
Manon Rheaume became the first woman to play in an NHL preseason game, appearing in net for the Tampa Bay Lightning in 1992.
The Clarkson Cup, first awarded in 2009, was the championship trophy for professional women's hockey in Canada before the PWHL era.
Shannon Szabados became one of the first women to play professional men's hockey when she started games in the SPHL in 2014.
Bob Probert and Tie Domi were two of the most famous enforcers in NHL history, combining for nearly 8,000 penalty minutes.
Dave 'The Hammer' Schultz set the all-time single-season penalty minutes record with 472 PIM in 1974-75 with the Philadelphia Flyers.
The 'Enforcer Code' was an unwritten set of rules fighters followed, including not jumping an unwilling opponent and answering the bell when challenged.
Tiger Williams holds the all-time NHL career penalty minutes record with 3,966 PIM over 14 seasons.
Fighting has declined dramatically in the NHL — from over 0.65 fights per game in the 1980s to under 0.15 per game by the 2020s.
The 'Goon' is a term for a player whose primary role was fighting and intimidation, a role that has nearly vanished from modern hockey.
George Parros, once a feared enforcer, became the head of the NHL's Department of Player Safety after retiring.
Hockey Night in Canada, first broadcast on radio in 1931, is the longest-running sports broadcast in the world.
Don Cherry hosted 'Coach's Corner' on Hockey Night in Canada for 38 years and became one of the most recognizable figures in Canadian culture.
Shinny is informal, unstructured hockey played on outdoor rinks or frozen ponds, a beloved Canadian pastime.
Outdoor rink culture is central to Canadian hockey identity, with thousands of community-maintained rinks across the country.
'The Rink' in Canadian culture refers not just to ice surfaces but to the community gathering points that form the social heart of many small towns.
Tim Hortons, the coffee chain founded by the late NHL defenseman, is so connected to hockey culture that it sponsors minor hockey programs across Canada.
The Heritage Classic was the NHL's first outdoor regular-season game, played in Edmonton in 2003 between the Oilers and Canadiens.
Connor McDavid scored 153 points in the 2022-23 season, the highest point total by any NHL player since Mario Lemieux's 161 in 1995-96.
Connor McDavid won the Conn Smythe Trophy in 2024 despite the Edmonton Oilers losing in the Stanley Cup Final to the Florida Panthers.
Jack Hughes scored 43 goals in the 2023-24 season, establishing himself as one of the top goal-scoring centers in the league.
Matthew Tkachuk led the Panthers in playoff scoring during their 2024 Stanley Cup championship run with clutch performances throughout.
Brady Tkachuk and the Ottawa Senators have been building toward playoff contention, with Brady scoring over 30 goals in multiple seasons.
John Tavares scored his 400th career NHL goal with the Maple Leafs, cementing his legacy as one of the top centers of his era.
Tyler Seguin's career has been marked by elite offensive production and resilience, returning from major hip surgery to continue starring in Dallas.
Auston Matthews won the Hart Trophy as NHL MVP in 2022, the first Maple Leafs player to win the award since Ted Kennedy in 1955.
The Fenway Park Winter Classic in 2010 between the Bruins and Flyers drew massive TV ratings and helped popularize outdoor NHL games.
The NHL Stadium Series has featured games at iconic venues like Yankee Stadium, Soldier Field, and the U.S. Air Force Academy.
Rogers Place in Edmonton, opened in 2016, features a massive center-hung scoreboard and world-class facilities for the Oilers.
The intimidating 'C of Red' at the Saddledome refers to Calgary Flames fans wearing red jerseys en masse during playoff games.
The 'Whiteout' tradition in Winnipeg sees Jets fans wearing white to playoff games, creating one of the most electric atmospheres in the NHL.
Ed Belfour, nicknamed 'Eddie the Eagle,' won 484 career games and was known for his intense competitiveness and acrobatic style.
Tuukka Rask led the Boston Bruins to the 2019 Stanley Cup Final and posted a .934 save percentage in that postseason run.
Mike Richter's 1994 playoff performance included a spectacular save on Pavel Bure's penalty shot in the Stanley Cup Final.
Glenn Hall invented the butterfly goaltending style and won the Conn Smythe Trophy in 1968 despite the Blues losing the Cup.
Tony Esposito recorded 76 career shutouts and posted a remarkable 2.92 career GAA across 16 NHL seasons.
The 2022 Colorado Avalanche dominated the playoffs, defeating the Tampa Bay Lightning in 6 games to end their bid for a three-peat.
The 1971 Canadiens upset saw rookie Ken Dryden lead Montreal past the heavily favored Boston Bruins in the first round.
The 2001 Colorado-New Jersey Stanley Cup Final featured Patrick Roy vs. Martin Brodeur, two of the greatest goalies ever, in an epic 7-game series.
The 1975 Flyers became the first expansion-era team to win the Stanley Cup, repeating the feat in 1975 with their physical 'Broad Street Bullies' style.
The 2010 Blackhawks ended a 49-year Stanley Cup drought for Chicago, sparking a new era of hockey popularity in the city.
Scotty Bowman's coaching record of 1,244 career regular-season wins stood for decades as the all-time benchmark.
The 'Russian Five' — Fedorov, Larionov, Kozlov, Konstantinov, and Fetisov — revolutionized Detroit Red Wings hockey in the late 1990s.
Vladimir Konstantinov's career was tragically ended by a limousine accident just days after the Red Wings' 1997 Stanley Cup victory.
The NHL's first outdoor game was the 2003 Heritage Classic in Edmonton, played before 57,167 fans at Commonwealth Stadium.
Patrice Bergeron won the Selke Trophy as the NHL's best defensive forward a record five times during his career with the Boston Bruins.
The 1980 'Miracle on Ice' U.S. team was coached by Herb Brooks, who was the last player cut from the 1960 gold medal team.
The longest overtime game in modern NHL history was a 2020 first-round playoff game between Tampa Bay and Columbus, lasting five overtimes.
Patrick Roy's 'wink' at Tomas Sandstrom during the 1993 playoffs became one of the most iconic moments of goaltending bravado.
The 2004 World Cup of Hockey was the last major international best-on-best tournament before the 2016 edition, a 12-year gap.
The NHL introduced video review for goals beginning in 1991, one of the first major sports leagues to use instant replay technology.
The 'Michigan' or lacrosse-style goal — scooping the puck on the stick blade and scoring from behind the net — was popularized by Mike Legg in 1996.
Andrei Svechnikov became the first player to score a lacrosse-style 'Michigan' goal in an NHL game in 2019.
The NHL has had three major work stoppages: the 1994-95 lockout (shortened season), the 2004-05 lockout (cancelled season), and the 2012-13 lockout (shortened season).
The original Winnipeg Jets franchise featured stars like Dale Hawerchuk and Teemu Selanne before relocating to Phoenix in 1996.
The Nashville Predators joined the NHL in 1998 and reached the Stanley Cup Final for the first time in 2017.
The Columbus Blue Jackets and Minnesota Wild joined the NHL together in 2000 as expansion teams in the league's most recent pre-Vegas expansion.
Kirill Kaprizov became the first true superstar in Minnesota Wild history, winning the Calder Trophy in 2021 and becoming a perennial All-Star.
Wayne Gretzky once said, 'Skate to where the puck is going, not where it has been,' one of the most quoted lines in sports.
The 'Gordie Howe hat trick' is actually quite rare for Gordie Howe himself — he only recorded two in his career.
The 1999 Stanley Cup-winning goal by Brett Hull remains controversial because his skate appeared to be in the crease, violating the rule at the time.
The 2007 Anaheim Ducks won the franchise's first Stanley Cup, led by captain Scott Niedermayer and Conn Smythe winner Jean-Sebastien Giguere.
The San Jose Sharks, founded in 1991, have never won a Stanley Cup but reached the Final in 2016, losing to the Pittsburgh Penguins.
Mike Modano is the all-time leading American-born point scorer in NHL history with 1,374 career points.
Artemi Panarin, nicknamed 'The Breadman,' went undrafted and played in the KHL before becoming an elite NHL playmaker with the Rangers.
The NHL's first Black player was Willie O'Ree, who debuted for the Boston Bruins on January 18, 1958, and the league now celebrates January 18 as Willie O'Ree Day.
NHL goaltenders typically lose 5-10 pounds during a game and drink up to 20 bottles of water to stay hydrated.
Jacques Plante was not only the first goalie to regularly wear a mask but also one of the first to roam from his crease to play the puck.
The 'Broadmoor Trophy' was awarded to the NCAA Division I men's ice hockey champion before the tournament trophy was simply called the championship trophy.
College hockey has produced NHL stars like Jack Eichel (Boston University), Cale Makar (UMass), and Zach Werenski (Michigan).
The Frozen Four is the NCAA Division I men's ice hockey championship tournament, held annually in April.
The NHL's first commissioner was Gary Bettman, who took office in 1993 and oversaw the league's growth from 24 to 32 teams.
The Arizona Coyotes relocated to Salt Lake City in 2024, becoming the Utah Hockey Club after years of arena and financial struggles in the desert.
Nikita Kucherov registered 144 points in 2018-19, the highest single-season point total in the NHL since Mario Lemieux's 161 in 1995-96 at the time.
The Montreal Forum, demolished in 2000, hosted more Stanley Cup celebrations than any other arena in hockey history.
The Battle of Alberta between the Calgary Flames and Edmonton Oilers is one of hockey's fiercest rivalries, reignited in the 2022 playoffs.
The Rangers-Islanders-Devils tri-state rivalry in New York and New Jersey creates some of the most intense fan atmospheres in the NHL.
Goaltender Martin Brodeur also holds the NHL record for most shutouts in a career with 125.
The NHL's Heritage Classic and Winter Classic outdoor games regularly draw TV audiences of over 3 million viewers in the United States.
Alexander Mogilny defected from the Soviet Union in 1989 to play in the NHL, one of the first Soviet players to do so.
The 2018 Vegas Golden Knights played the 'Golden Misfit' narrative, using players from the expansion draft that other teams left unprotected.
Hockey sticks were entirely one-piece wood until Stan Mikita and teammate Bobby Hull accidentally discovered that a curved blade improved shot power and deception.
Goaltender equipment can weigh over 40 pounds total, including leg pads, chest protector, helmet, blocker, trapper, and skates.
The New York Islanders won four consecutive Stanley Cups from 1980 to 1983, a dynasty led by Mike Bossy, Bryan Trottier, and Denis Potvin.
The 'Fog Game' of 1988 in the Stanley Cup Final between Boston and Edmonton saw the game halted multiple times due to fog covering the ice at Boston Garden.
The NHL's first expansion in 1967 added the Philadelphia Flyers, St. Louis Blues, Minnesota North Stars, Los Angeles Kings, Pittsburgh Penguins, and Oakland Seals.
Darryl Sutter coached the Los Angeles Kings to back-to-back Stanley Cup titles in 2012 and 2014 and later won again coaching the Calgary Flames' rivals.
The Richard Riot of 1955 erupted in Montreal after Maurice 'Rocket' Richard was suspended for the remainder of the season and playoffs.
Connor McDavid led the Edmonton Oilers on a historic playoff rally in 2024, coming back from 3-0 down against the Florida Panthers before falling in Game 7.
Nathan MacKinnon was the first overall pick in the 2013 NHL Draft and won the Calder Trophy as rookie of the year that season.
Auston Matthews grew up in Scottsdale, Arizona, making him one of the most prominent NHL stars to come from a non-traditional hockey market.
Leon Draisaitl and Connor McDavid formed one of the most dominant 1-2 center combinations in NHL history with the Edmonton Oilers.
The Gagarin Cup is the KHL's championship trophy, named after Soviet cosmonaut Yuri Gagarin.
The NHL introduced the 'Coach's Challenge' in 2015-16, allowing coaches to challenge goals for offside or goaltender interference.
Terry Sawchuk's death at age 40 in 1970 was ruled accidental after an altercation with teammate Ron Stewart, adding a tragic element to his legacy.
Glenn Hall was famous for being so nervous before games that he would routinely vomit, yet still played with remarkable consistency.
The 'Miracle on Manchester' in 1982 saw the Los Angeles Kings overcome a 5-0 deficit against the Edmonton Oilers in the third period to win in overtime.
The PWHL's first-ever championship was won by Minnesota in 2024, marking a milestone in the growth of professional women's hockey.
John Tavares was granted 'exceptional status' to play in the OHL at age 14, only the second player ever given that designation after Connor McDavid.
Hockey visors became increasingly popular in the 2000s and were mandated for all new NHL players starting in 2013-14.
The Bettman era has seen the NHL grow from a $400 million business in 1993 to a multi-billion dollar league with 32 franchises.
